Exact likelihood formula for the linear birth-death process with destructive sampling

A.A. King

The following codes simulate a realization of the genealogy process induced by a linear birth-death-sampling process with birth rate \(\lambda\), death rate \(\mu\), non-destructive sampling rate \(\psi\), and destructive sampling rate \(\chi\).

true.params <- data.frame(lambda=3.5,mu=0.8,psi=1,chi=2,n0=20)
true.params |>
  with(
    runLBDP(lambda=lambda,mu=mu,psi=psi,chi=chi,n0=n0,time=3)
  ) -> x

The following designs a slice through parameter space in the direction of the parameter \(r=\chi/(\psi+\chi)\), which is the fraction of the samples that are destructive.

expand_grid(
  lambda=3.5,
  mu=0.8,
  r=seq(0,1,by=0.01),
  totsamp=with(true.params,psi+chi),
  n0=20
  ) |>
  mutate(
    psi=totsamp*(1-r),
    chi=totsamp*r
  ) -> params

Next, we perform a sequential Monte Carlo (particle filter) computation at each point in the above design.

library(doParallel)
library(iterators)
foreach (
  p=iter(params,"row"),
  .inorder=FALSE,
  .combine=bind_rows
) %dopar% {
  p |>
    with(
      x |>
        lbdp_exact(lambda=lambda,mu=mu,psi=psi,chi=chi,n0=n0)
    ) -> ll1
  bind_cols(p,exact=ll1)
} |>
  bake(file="slice1.rds") -> params

expand_grid(
  lambda=3.5,
  mu=0.8,
  psi=exp(seq(log(0.1),log(8),length=100)),
  chi=exp(seq(log(0.1),log(8),length=100)),
  n0=20
) -> params
library(doParallel)
library(iterators)
registerDoParallel(cores=20)
foreach (
  p=iter(params,"row"),
  .inorder=FALSE,
  .combine=bind_rows
) %dopar% {
  p |>
    with(
      x |>
        lbdp_exact(lambda=lambda,mu=mu,psi=psi,chi=chi,n0=n0)
    ) -> ll1
  bind_cols(p,exact=ll1)
} |>
  bake(file="slice2.rds",dependson=params) -> params

References

Gavryushkina A, Welch D, Stadler T, Drummond AJ (2014). “Bayesian Inference of Sampled Ancestor Trees for Epidemiology and Fossil Calibration.” PLOS Computational Biology, 10(12), e1003919. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003919.
King AA, Lin Q, Ionides EL (2022). “Markov Genealogy Processes.” Theoretical Population Biology, 143, 77–91. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.tpb.2021.11.003.
King AA, Lin Q, Ionides EL (2024). “Exact Phylodynamic Likelihood via Structured Markov Genealogy Processes.” arXiv,. https://doi.org/10.48550/arxiv.2405.17032.
Stadler T (2010). “Sampling-Through-Time in Birth-Death Trees.” Journal of Theoretical Biology, 267(3), 396–404. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jtbi.2010.09.010.
